questionHtml: Use your result from part (f)(ii) to show that the equation x4-2x3+3x2-4x+5=0 has at least one complex root.
markschemeHtml:
4<6  OR  22-2×3<0          R1

hence there is at least one complex root.         AG

 

Note: Allow FT from part (f)(ii) for the R mark provided numerical reasoning is seen.

 

[1 mark]

meta.title: Use your result from part (f)(ii) to show that the equation x4-2x3+3x2-4x+5=0 has at least one complex root.
